services.name: AWS Lambda
services.description: Run code in response to events without provisioning or managing servers, paying only for execution time.
keyUseCases: Event-driven processing triggered by S3, SQS, API Gateway, DynamoDB Streams, or EventBridge
Backend logic for serverless APIs and microservices
Data transformation and enrichment in streaming pipelines
Scheduled automation tasks and operational scripts

services.name: AWS Fargate (ECS)
services.description: Run containers without managing the underlying servers or clusters — define CPU and memory and Fargate handles the rest.
keyUseCases: Long-running containerised workloads not suited to Lambda's 15-minute limit
Microservices that require custom dependencies, native libraries, or specific runtimes
Batch processing jobs with variable resource requirements
Moving existing Docker workloads to a serverless container environment

services.name: Amazon EC2
services.description: Virtual servers in the cloud with full operating system control, available in hundreds of instance types.
keyUseCases: Workloads requiring specific hardware, OS configuration, or legacy software
High-performance computing with GPU or HPC instance families
Spot Instance fleets for cost-optimised batch or rendering workloads
Stateful applications that cannot run in containers or serverless environments

services.name: AWS Batch
services.description: Run large-scale batch computing jobs across managed EC2 or Fargate compute environments without managing job schedulers.
keyUseCases: High-throughput batch jobs such as video transcoding, genomics, or financial simulations
Overnight jobs that benefit from Spot Instance pricing for significant cost savings
Data processing pipelines with dependencies between jobs, managed by an automatic queue
Large-scale scientific computing that needs multiple machines working in parallel

services.name: AWS Elemental MediaConvert
services.description: Transcode video files at scale into multiple formats and resolutions using a fully managed, pay-per-minute service.
keyUseCases: Multi-resolution video transcoding for adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS, DASH)
Broadcast-grade file-based video processing with professional codec support
Automated video pipeline triggered by S3 object events
Caption and subtitle generation and embedding for accessibility

services.name: Amazon S3
services.description: Highly durable object storage for any amount of data, accessible from anywhere via HTTPS.
keyUseCases: Data lake storage for analytics pipelines — store raw, processed, and curated data
Backup, archive, and disaster recovery with lifecycle policies and Glacier tiering
Input and output staging for asynchronous processing pipelines such as video upload and transcode
Pre-signed URL uploads for secure direct browser-to-S3 file uploads without a proxy

services.name: Amazon EFS
services.description: Managed shared file system that multiple servers and containers can read from and write to simultaneously — scales automatically as data grows.
keyUseCases: Shared media or asset storage for web applications running across multiple servers
Content management systems where multiple instances need to access the same files
Machine learning training jobs sharing a large dataset across worker nodes
Persistent shared storage for containers that need to read and write the same files

services.name: Amazon EBS
services.description: Persistent disk storage attached to a single EC2 instance — like an internal hard drive in the cloud with configurable speed and size.
keyUseCases: Database storage for self-managed databases on EC2 such as PostgreSQL or MySQL
Boot volumes and OS disks for EC2 instances
High-performance storage for latency-sensitive applications that need fast disk access
Point-in-time snapshots for backup and cross-region disaster recovery

services.name: Amazon DynamoDB
services.description: Fully managed key-value and document database delivering single-digit millisecond reads and writes at any scale — no servers to provision or patch.
keyUseCases: Session state, user profiles, and preference storage for high-concurrency applications
Audit log and event history storage with automatic expiry using Time to Live
Gaming leaderboards, IoT device state, and real-time voting systems
Global Tables for multi-region active-active replication without managing replicas

services.name: Amazon Aurora
services.description: MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built for the cloud, offering up to 5x throughput of standard MySQL.
keyUseCases: Transactional workloads requiring relational data integrity and complex SQL queries
Aurora Serverless v2 for unpredictable workloads that need to scale to zero between bursts
Global Database for low-latency multi-region reads with fast cross-region replication
Replacing self-managed RDS for higher availability with automatic 6-way replication across 3 AZs

services.name: Amazon ElastiCache
services.description: Managed in-memory data store for sub-millisecond read performance — compatible with Redis and Memcached, sitting in front of your database.
keyUseCases: Database query caching to reduce read load and response times for popular data
Session store for stateless web applications that need fast distributed session lookup
Rate limiting and counter operations using in-memory atomic increments
Lightweight pub/sub message distribution within a single region

services.name: Amazon Timestream
services.description: Serverless database purpose-built for time-stamped data such as sensor readings and metrics — automatically tiers data from fast storage to cheap archive.
keyUseCases: IoT sensor telemetry — temperature, pressure, vibration — with built-in time-based queries
Application performance metrics and monitoring data with automatic aggregation
Financial tick data and event-rate analytics using time-window queries
Drop-in replacement for self-managed time-series databases with zero server management

services.name: Amazon RDS
services.description: Managed relational database service supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, and SQL Server.
keyUseCases: Lift-and-shift of existing relational workloads with minimal code changes
Read Replicas to offload reporting and analytics queries from the primary database
Multi-AZ deployment for automatic failover and minimal downtime during a failure
Oracle or SQL Server workloads that cannot migrate to an Aurora-compatible engine

services.name: Amazon SQS
services.description: Fully managed message queuing service for decoupling producers from consumers and absorbing traffic bursts.
keyUseCases: Decoupling microservices so a slow consumer does not block the upstream producer
Absorbing burst traffic — SQS buffers requests while downstream processors catch up
Dead-letter queues to capture and investigate failed message processing
FIFO queues for exactly-once processing and strict ordering guarantees

services.name: Amazon SNS
services.description: Fully managed pub/sub messaging for fan-out notifications to multiple subscribers simultaneously.
keyUseCases: Fan-out a single event to multiple SQS queues, Lambda functions, or HTTP endpoints in parallel
Mobile push notifications to iOS, Android, and web endpoints
Operational alerts — send SMS or email notifications when CloudWatch alarms trigger
Cross-application event broadcasting where multiple consumers need the same event

services.name: Amazon EventBridge
services.description: Serverless event bus that routes events between AWS services, custom applications, and SaaS partners using content-based rules.
keyUseCases: Decoupled event-driven architectures where producers and consumers evolve independently
Scheduled rules (cron) for periodic Lambda invocations without managing cron infrastructure
SaaS integrations — receive events from Salesforce, Zendesk, or Datadog without polling
Event replay and archiving to reprocess historical events after consumer failures

services.name: AWS Step Functions
services.description: Visual serverless workflow orchestration for coordinating Lambda functions, AWS services, and human approval steps.
keyUseCases: Multi-step business workflows with conditional branching, retries, and parallel execution
Long-running processes (hours to days) with built-in wait states for human approvals
Replacing complex error-handling and retry logic in Lambda with declarative state machines
Saga pattern implementation for distributed transaction coordination across microservices

services.name: Amazon API Gateway
services.description: Fully managed service for creating, publishing, and securing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs at any scale.
keyUseCases: Serverless API front-end backed by Lambda — no servers to manage or scale
JWT and IAM authorization at the edge before requests reach backend services
Usage plans and API keys for per-consumer rate limiting and quota enforcement
WebSocket APIs for real-time two-way communication in chat or live data applications

services.name: AWS AppSync
services.description: Managed API service using GraphQL — a query language that lets clients request exactly the data they need, with built-in real-time subscriptions.
keyUseCases: Single API endpoint that aggregates data from multiple backend services into one response
Real-time collaborative features such as live feeds, chat, and shared dashboards
Mobile and web apps that need offline data sync and automatic conflict resolution
Rapid API development when teams prefer a schema-first, flexible query model

services.name: AWS IoT Core
services.description: Managed message broker that connects IoT devices to the cloud over lightweight protocols — handles authentication, routing, and scaling for millions of devices.
keyUseCases: Connect sensors, cameras, and embedded devices to AWS without managing broker infrastructure
Route device messages to Kinesis, Lambda, S3, or DynamoDB using no-code rules
Authenticate each device individually using X.509 certificates for strong security
Device Shadow to store and retrieve the last-known state of a device even when offline

services.name: Amazon Connect
services.description: Cloud-based contact centre for voice, chat, and task routing — set up a full call centre in minutes with no hardware or telephony contracts.
keyUseCases: Customer support contact centre with voice and chat without purchasing telephony hardware
Multi-region failover for contact centres that cannot afford downtime during a regional outage
Skill-based routing to direct customers to agents with the right language or product expertise
Integration with CRM systems and custom backends via Lambda flows during a call

services.name: Amazon SES
services.description: Managed email sending service for transactional, notification, and marketing emails — high deliverability at low cost without managing mail servers.
keyUseCases: Transactional emails such as order confirmations, password resets, and welcome messages
Application-triggered notifications when combined with Lambda or Step Functions
Bulk email campaigns with built-in suppression lists and bounce handling
Inbound email processing routed to S3, Lambda, or SNS for automated reply handling

services.name: AWS IAM
services.description: Identity and access management for controlling who can do what with AWS resources using policies and roles.
keyUseCases: Least-privilege IAM roles for Lambda, EC2, and ECS tasks scoped to only the resources they need
Cross-account access using role assumption for shared services and centralised logging
Service control policies (SCPs) in AWS Organisations to enforce guardrails across all accounts
IAM Identity Centre (SSO) for centralised human access management across multiple AWS accounts

services.name: Amazon Cognito
services.description: Managed user authentication and authorization service supporting JWT tokens, OAuth 2.0, SAML, and social sign-in.
keyUseCases: User pools for application sign-up, sign-in, and JWT token issuance without a custom auth server
API Gateway JWT authorizer integration for validating tokens at the API layer
Federated identity for allowing users to sign in with Google, Apple, or corporate SAML IdPs
Hosted UI for a ready-made sign-in page with no frontend code required

services.name: AWS KMS
services.description: Managed key management service for creating and controlling encryption keys used across AWS services.
keyUseCases: Envelope encryption for S3, DynamoDB, RDS, EBS, Kinesis, and Lambda environment variables
Customer-managed keys (CMKs) for fine-grained control over key rotation and access policies
Audit key usage via CloudTrail integration — every encrypt/decrypt call is logged
Cross-account key sharing for decrypting data shared between AWS accounts

services.name: AWS Secrets Manager
services.description: Store, rotate, and retrieve database credentials, API keys, and other secrets with automatic rotation support.
keyUseCases: Database password rotation on a schedule without application downtime (RDS native integration)
Securely injecting third-party API keys into Lambda environment variables at invocation time
Centralised secrets management across multiple AWS accounts and regions
Replacing hard-coded credentials in application configuration with programmatic retrieval

services.name: AWS WAF
services.description: Web application firewall that filters malicious HTTP traffic before it reaches your application.
keyUseCases: Blocking common web attacks — SQL injection, cross-site scripting — at the API Gateway or ALB layer
Rate-based rules to automatically throttle IPs sending excessive requests
AWS Managed Rules for instant protection against known threat patterns maintained by AWS
Geo-blocking to restrict API access to specific countries or regions

services.name: AWS Certificate Manager
services.description: Provision, manage, and auto-renew TLS certificates for use with AWS services at no cost.
keyUseCases: Free TLS certificates for API Gateway, ALB, and CloudFront without manual renewal
Wildcard certificates covering all subdomains of a domain in a single certificate
Private CA for issuing internal certificates for service-to-service mTLS communication
Certificate transparency logging for auditing all issued certificates

services.name: Amazon VPC
services.description: Your own private section of the AWS network where you control IP ranges, subnets, routing rules, and what can access the internet.
keyUseCases: Isolating workloads into private subnets inaccessible from the internet
Security groups as stateful instance-level firewalls for fine-grained traffic control
VPC endpoints for private connectivity to AWS services without traversing the internet
VPC peering or Transit Gateway for connecting multiple VPCs across accounts or regions

services.name: Amazon Route 53
services.description: Scalable DNS and traffic routing service with health checks, failover, and latency-based routing.
keyUseCases: Health-check-based failover routing between primary and secondary endpoints or regions
Latency-based routing to direct users to the closest AWS region for lowest response time
Weighted routing for canary deployments — gradually shifting traffic to a new version
Private hosted zones for internal DNS resolution within a VPC

services.name: AWS Global Accelerator
services.description: Improve availability and performance for global applications by routing traffic through AWS's private backbone network.
keyUseCases: TCP/UDP workloads (gaming, IoT, VoIP) requiring static Anycast IP addresses and network-layer routing
Instant failover across regions at the network layer — faster than DNS TTL-based failover
Reducing last-mile latency for global users by entering the AWS backbone as close to them as possible
Non-HTTP workloads that cannot use CloudFront's application-layer acceleration

services.name: AWS PrivateLink
services.description: Connect services between VPCs or AWS accounts over private AWS networking without sending traffic across the public internet.
keyUseCases: SaaS providers exposing services to customers' VPCs without VPC peering or internet exposure
Private access to AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, SQS) from within a VPC without a NAT Gateway
Cross-account service sharing within an organisation without complex VPC peering mesh
Compliance requirements prohibiting data from traversing the public internet

services.name: Elastic Load Balancing
services.description: Automatically distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets — EC2 instances, containers, Lambda functions, or IP addresses — across Availability Zones.
keyUseCases: Application Load Balancer (ALB) for HTTP/HTTPS traffic with path-based and host-based routing
Network Load Balancer (NLB) for ultra-low-latency TCP/UDP traffic handling millions of requests per second
Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB) for deploying third-party virtual appliances such as firewalls at scale
Health checks and automatic failover to healthy targets for high availability

services.name: Amazon CloudFront
services.description: Global content delivery network (CDN) that caches and delivers content from edge locations closest to users for low-latency access.
keyUseCases: Accelerating static and dynamic web content delivery with global edge caching
HTTPS termination and DDoS protection at the edge with AWS Shield integration
Origin failover to a secondary origin for high availability during origin outages
Lambda@Edge and CloudFront Functions for request/response manipulation at the edge

services.name: NAT Gateway
services.description: Managed network address translation service that enables instances in private subnets to connect to the internet while preventing inbound connections.
keyUseCases: Allowing Lambda functions or containers in private subnets to reach external APIs and package registries
Outbound internet access for private EC2 instances to download patches and updates
Replacing self-managed NAT instances with a fully managed, highly available alternative
Per-AZ deployment for fault-tolerant outbound connectivity

services.name: Amazon CloudWatch
services.description: Unified monitoring and observability service for metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards across all AWS services.
keyUseCases: Alarms on Lambda error rate, API Gateway 5XX rate, SQS queue depth, and DynamoDB throttles
Log Insights for ad-hoc querying of structured and unstructured application logs
Dashboards for operational visibility into the health of a multi-service application
Container Insights and Lambda Insights for enhanced performance metrics without code changes

services.name: AWS X-Ray
services.description: Distributed tracing service that maps requests as they travel through microservices and AWS managed services.
keyUseCases: Identifying latency bottlenecks across a chain of Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB calls
Root-cause analysis of intermittent errors in distributed systems
Sampling rules to capture a representative trace sample without tracing 100% of requests
Service map visualisation showing dependencies and error rates between services

services.name: AWS CloudTrail
services.description: Records all API calls made in an AWS account for compliance, security auditing, and operational troubleshooting.
keyUseCases: Audit log of all AWS resource changes — who created, modified, or deleted what and when
Security incident investigation — trace the exact sequence of API calls during a suspected breach
Compliance requirements (PCI DSS, SOC 2, HIPAA) requiring an immutable API activity log
CloudTrail Lake for SQL-based querying of multi-account, multi-region API history

services.name: AWS Config
services.description: Continuously records changes to your AWS resources and checks whether they comply with your rules — alerts or auto-remediates when something drifts.
keyUseCases: Detecting configuration drift — e.g. a security group that was modified to allow 0.0.0.0/0
Compliance rules to enforce encryption-at-rest on all EBS volumes or S3 buckets
Configuration change history for auditing the state of resources over time
Automatic remediation using Systems Manager Automation when a rule violation is detected

services.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
services.description: Real-time data streaming service that ingests high-throughput event data from many producers and delivers it to multiple consumers in parallel.
keyUseCases: Ingest millions of events per second from web, mobile, IoT, or application sources
Fan out the same stream to multiple consumers — analytics, alerting, archiving — simultaneously
Replay events for up to 7 days to recover from a failed consumer without data loss
Buffer traffic spikes so downstream processors are not overwhelmed

services.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
services.description: Automatically batches and delivers streaming data to S3, Redshift, OpenSearch, or Splunk on a schedule — zero code required for the delivery layer.
keyUseCases: Zero-code delivery of streaming data to S3 for data lake ingestion within 60 seconds
Automatic format conversion (JSON to Parquet or ORC) and compression before S3 delivery
Direct streaming delivery to Redshift or OpenSearch without intermediate Lambda consumers
Enriching records in-flight using a Lambda transform function before delivery

services.name: AWS Glue
services.description: Serverless service for moving, transforming, and cataloguing data between stores — discovers schemas automatically and runs jobs without managing servers.
keyUseCases: Discover and register the schema of data sitting in S3 so Athena can query it
Transform raw data into a cleaner format before loading it into a data warehouse
Schedule recurring data pipeline jobs without managing infrastructure
Build a central data catalogue so analysts can find and understand available datasets

services.name: Amazon Athena
services.description: Serverless SQL query engine for analysing data in S3 using standard SQL — pay per query, no clusters to manage.
keyUseCases: Ad-hoc SQL queries on structured and semi-structured data (CSV, JSON, Parquet) stored in S3
Log analysis — query VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, and ALB access logs stored in S3
Data exploration before committing to a data warehouse for recurring structured analytics
Federated queries across S3, DynamoDB, and on-premises data sources in a single SQL statement

services.name: Amazon Redshift
services.description: Cloud data warehouse for running complex analytical queries on petabyte-scale structured datasets with columnar storage.
keyUseCases: Business intelligence and dashboarding requiring complex multi-table joins and aggregations
Redshift Serverless for variable analytical workloads that scale to zero between query bursts
Data sharing — share live data across Redshift clusters without copying or moving data
Redshift Spectrum for querying S3 data lake tables alongside Redshift-managed tables in a single query

services.name: Amazon OpenSearch Service
services.description: Managed OpenSearch and Elasticsearch cluster for full-text search, log analytics, and real-time data exploration.
keyUseCases: Full-text search with relevance ranking for product catalogues, document stores, or knowledge bases
Log aggregation and dashboarding — a managed alternative to self-hosted ELK stacks
Real-time dashboards for operational data streams (e.g. application metrics, security events)
Anomaly detection using built-in machine learning on time-series metrics
